groups.title: Foreground
groups.description: Navigation foreground colors for text and icons

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.fg_base
tokens.description: Navigation foreground — text. Always uses the highest contrast cobalt value; navigation text is black regardless of lightness mode.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.fg_disabled
tokens.description: Navigation foreground — disabled. Muted text/icon color using the universal cobalt disabled scale.

groups.title: Background Base
groups.description: Navigation background colors at different contrast levels

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.bg_base.contrast_high
tokens.description: Navigation background — high contrast. Applies where a strong surface fill is required (e.g. pressed, selected).

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.bg_base.contrast_medium
tokens.description: Navigation background — medium contrast. Applies in hover and focus states for a visible but non-dominant surface.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.bg_base.contrast_low
tokens.description: Navigation background — low contrast. Applies in enabled state for a subtle surface that does not dominate the layout.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.bg_disabled.solid
tokens.description: Navigation background — disabled solid. Opaque muted surface for disabled navigation items.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.bg_disabled.opacity
tokens.description: Navigation background — disabled opacity. Semi-transparent muted surface for disabled navigation items.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.border_base.brand
tokens.description: Navigation border — brand. Uses ob.p.color.basic.federal_red. Applied to selected and pressed states as the primary brand indicator line.

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.border_base.subtle
tokens.description: Navigation border — subtle. Lighter red shade for non-active borders (enabled, hover, focus states).

tokens.name: ob.s1.color.navigation.border_base.disabled
tokens.description: Navigation border — disabled. Muted border using the cobalt_alpha disabled scale.
